entries.note: Plain-language tour of what tracing a model's thoughts reveals; gentlest entry point.
Field map of methods, results, and unsolved problems from leading practitioners.
The founding manifesto: features and circuits as the units of network anatomy.
Defines the working vocabulary: residual stream, head composition, induction heads.
Evidence that induction heads drive in-context learning in real transformers.
Why neurons mean many things at once: features compressed in superposition.
First demonstration that sparse autoencoders decompose a transformer into interpretable features.
Interpretable features at frontier scale, including the famous Golden Gate Bridge feature.
Open sparse autoencoders on Gemma 2; the kit for hands-on practice.
Complete reverse-engineering of a learned algorithm, explaining how grokking happens.
The template circuit analysis: activation patching dissects a GPT-2 task.
Attribution graphs and transcoders: the current method for tracing computations.
Unsupervised probes recover a model's internal notion of truth.
Steering behavior with contrastive activation vectors added at inference time.
Refusal occupies a single direction you can remove or add.
Microscope case studies in Claude: planning ahead, multilingual circuits, unfaithful reasoning.
Blind teams use interpretability to uncover a model's hidden training objective.
Linear directions that monitor and steer character traits like sycophancy.
The standard field map: RLHF, scalable oversight, and assurance in one survey.
Accessible book-length introduction to why aligning ML with human values is hard.
DeepMind's philosophical framing of what we should align AI to, and for whom.
Formalizes value alignment as a cooperative game where machines learn human preferences.
Origin of scalable oversight: agents debate so humans can judge superhuman work.
RLHF proven on real language tasks; the direct precursor to InstructGPT.
Anthropic's helpful-honest-harmless framing that made chat assistants the alignment testbed.
InstructGPT: the RLHF recipe behind ChatGPT and every modern aligned assistant.
Anthropic's HH-RLHF: the helpful-versus-harmless tradeoff measured at scale.
Align with written principles and AI feedback instead of human harm labels.
DPO: preference tuning without RL; now the default open-model alignment method.
Can weak supervisors elicit strong models' abilities? Core superalignment experiment.
Implanted deceptive behavior survives standard safety training — a landmark warning.
First evidence a model strategically fakes alignment to preserve its preferences.
Emergent misalignment: finetuning on insecure code yields broadly malicious behavior.
Behavioral traits pass between models through seemingly innocuous training data.
Reward hacking in real training generalizes to sabotage; tested mitigations included.
DeepMind tests whether deployed models actually obey their written principles.
A structured map of everything that can go wrong with language models.
Readable book tracing how ML systems drift from human intent; the field's best on-ramp.
Concise map of the competing technical proposals for building safe advanced AI.
Founding scalable-oversight proposal: models debate, humans judge, to supervise superhuman systems.
Beloved anecdote collection of AI gaming its objectives; makes specification failure vivid.
Shows agents can learn the wrong goal even under a correct reward.
Established systematic red-teaming of LLMs; methods and lessons still used today.
Defined dangerous-capability evaluations, now standard practice at every frontier lab.
Backdoored deceptive behavior survives safety training; the landmark deceptive-alignment result.
Models generalize from simple sycophancy to tampering with their own reward.
First evidence a model strategically fakes alignment in training to protect its preferences.
Founded AI control: protocols that stay safe even if models actively scheme.
Finetuning on insecure code makes models broadly malicious: emergent misalignment.
Blind auditing game: can teams uncover a model's deliberately hidden objective?
Chain-of-thought often hides true reasoning, limiting CoT-based safety monitoring.
Models resort to blackmail and leaks in simulated corporate stress tests.
Behavioral traits pass between models through innocuous-looking training data.
Reward hacking in production RL naturally produced broad misalignment, plus a mitigation.
Maps the o1-era: RL and test-time compute turning LLMs into reasoners.
Catalogs prompting-era techniques: chain-of-thought, decomposition, self-verification.
GPT-f, the first serious LLM theorem prover; opened the formal-math line.
Introduces GSM8K and trained verifiers — the seed of process supervision.
Nature landmark: AI guides mathematicians to genuinely new theorems.
"Let's think step by step": chain-of-thought works without any examples.
Massive sampling plus filtering reaches median-human level in coding contests.
Rewarding each reasoning step beats rewarding only final answers on math.
Defines the CoT faithfulness problem: explanations can rationalize biased answers.
Cold water: models can't fix their own reasoning without external feedback.
FunSearch: an LLM plus an evaluator discovers new mathematics.
The reasoning-model paradigm shift: RL-trained chain-of-thought, documented.
Why letting models think longer can beat training them bigger.
Anthropic measures CoT faithfulness in frontier reasoning models; it is low.
CoT monitoring catches reward hacking — unless you optimize against it.
AlphaProof: RL plus Lean reaches IMO silver-medal performance.
Models can't fully steer their own thinking — good news for monitorability.
Anthropic's candid tour of why measuring model behavior is surprisingly hard.
Maps how LLMs grade other LLMs, now the field's dominant evaluation method.
Orients you in agent benchmarks: web, code, tools, and how they're scored.
The pre-LLM benchmark suite that set the template for measuring language understanding.
The Codex paper; introduced HumanEval and pass@k, still the code-eval standard.
Shows models reproduce human misconceptions; the standard truthfulness benchmark.
BIG-bench: 200+ crowd-sourced tasks probing what scaling does and doesn't buy.
HELM: evaluate many models on many scenarios and metrics, systematically.
Anthropic uses models to write their own evaluations, scaling behavioral testing.
Argues 'emergent' abilities are artifacts of metric choice; sharpened eval methodology.
The agenda-setting case for testing frontier models for dangerous capabilities.
Med-PaLM: landmark domain evaluation, pairing exam benchmarks with physician judgment.
Google-proof PhD-level questions; the capability benchmark every frontier model reports.
DeepMind's concrete eval suite for cyber, persuasion, and self-proliferation risks.
Can agents replicate AI research papers? Rubric-graded frontier capability eval.
Physician-written rubrics grade realistic health conversations; the new evaluation style.
OpenAI argues benchmarks reward confident guessing, making hallucination an evaluation problem.
Benchmark saturation up close: why a flagship coding eval got retired.
Broad map of LLM safety, security, and privacy threats and defenses.
Taxonomy of red-teaming attacks on generative models; how practitioners probe for failures.
Explains why safety training fails: competing objectives and mismatched generalization behind jailbreaks.
Anthropic's methodology for red-teaming LLMs at scale, with what they learned.
Defines indirect prompt injection: hijacking LLM apps via poisoned retrieved content.
Shows LLMs memorize and leak verbatim training data, the core privacy attack.
Classic clean-label data poisoning: stealthy training-set attacks that flip model predictions.
The canonical method for watermarking LLM outputs to detect machine-generated text.
PAIR: automatically jailbreaks black-box models in roughly 20 queries using an attacker LLM.
One adversarial image jailbreaks aligned vision-language models; multimodal robustness is hard.
Backdoored deceptive behavior survives safety training, and adversarial training can hide it.
Refusal lives in one activation direction; ablating it removes safety guardrails.
Comprehensive trustworthiness benchmark for GPT models across eight safety and robustness dimensions.
Argues detection of AI text has fundamental limits as models improve.
DeepMind measures how jailbreak success scales with attacker effort and model size.
Tests whether models actually obey their written behavioral specs under pressure.
Trust, risk, and security framework for multi-agent LLM systems: the agentic frontier.
Current view of unlearning to remove harmful or private knowledge from LLMs.
Sweeping survey of LLM agents: brain, perception, action, and agent societies.
Compact taxonomy of how agents are built, applied, and evaluated.
Anthropic's practical playbook: simple composable patterns beat complex frameworks.
Pre-LLM milestone: multi-agent RL reaches grandmaster StarCraft.
Hide-and-seek agents invent tools; emergence from multi-agent competition.
First major LLM agent: web browsing plus human feedback.
Minecraft agent that writes code and grows a reusable skill library.
First broad benchmark of LLMs acting as agents across eight environments.
Agents as a software company: roles and procedures make collaboration work.
The human-validated coding benchmark that defined coding-agent progress.
How Claude learned to operate a computer from screenshots.
OpenAI's RL-trained GUI agent powering Operator.
Can agents do real ML engineering? Kaggle-style measurement.
The deep-research paradigm: agents that investigate autonomously for minutes.
Multi-agent Gemini system proposing novel, lab-validated scientific hypotheses.
Claude runs a real shop; where long-horizon autonomy breaks.
Stress tests show agents can turn insider threat under pressure.
When adding more agents helps, and when it hurts.
Broad, readable map of LLM training, architectures, and capabilities for newcomers.
Surveys the sparse mixture-of-experts methods powering efficient large-scale model training.
Catalogs LoRA, adapters, and cheap ways to fine-tune huge models.
GPT-3: the paper that launched the scaling era and few-shot prompting.
Loss falls as smooth power laws in compute, data, parameters.
Chinchilla: most big models are undertrained; balance parameters against data.
Original sparse MoE: huge capacity without huge per-token compute cost.
Classic puzzle: why over-parameterized nets generalize despite fitting random labels.
Argues new abilities appear abruptly only past certain model scales.
Counterargument: emergent jumps may be artifacts of discontinuous evaluation metrics.
InstructGPT: the RLHF recipe that aligns models to instructions.
Scaling instruction finetuning across tasks sharply improves zero-shot generalization.
DPO: replaces RLHF's reward model and RL with one simple loss.
Simplifies MoE routing to scale transformers toward trillion parameters.
Fine-grained and shared experts give better specialization per compute budget.
Open frontier report: MoE plus FP8 and efficiency tricks at scale.
Distillation can secretly transmit a teacher's behavioral traits through training data.
Fresh probe of persistent behavioral artifacts that training leaves behind.
Map of the whole multimodal-LLM landscape: architectures, training recipes, evaluation, open problems.
Readable tour of how multimodal LLMs emerged and what defines the current generation.
The cornerstone: contrastive image-text pretraining enabling open-vocabulary, zero-shot visual recognition.
Models text and image as one token stream; launched the text-to-image era.
Diffusion overtakes GANs for image synthesis—the engine behind modern text-to-image generation.
Learns joint video-text representations from narrated instructional video at web scale.
What actually matters when pretraining vision-language models: interleaved image-text data and recipes.
Adds grounding—linking words to image regions and bounding boxes—to multimodal LLMs.
Brings step-by-step chain-of-thought reasoning to problems combining images and text.
Scaling generative multimodal models unlocks in-context learning across images and text.
Treats speech as discrete tokens, enabling zero-shot text-to-speech from a short sample.
Anatomy of a flagship omni-model handling text, vision, and audio together.
Current-generation open vision-language model with dynamic any-resolution image handling.
The benchmark stress-testing how well multimodal LLMs actually understand video.
Where the field stands on measuring and reducing multimodal hallucination—the key reliability problem.
The push toward multimodal agents that see screens and operate software.
Friendly big-picture tour of deep RL ideas and why they work.
Maps RLHF end to end: preference data, reward models, policy optimization.
Orients you in the o1-style reinforced-reasoning wave reshaping the field.
AlphaStar: self-play multi-agent RL reaches grandmaster StarCraft II.
OpenAI Five: scaled-up self-play PPO beats Dota 2 world champions.
MuZero plans with a learned model, mastering Atari, Go, chess, shogi.
Sim-to-real RL with domain randomization controls a physical robot hand.
First RLHF applied to language models; the modern lineage starts here.
The InstructGPT recipe behind ChatGPT: supervised tuning, reward model, PPO.
Anthropic's helpful-and-harmless assistant trained with RLHF at production scale.
Drops the reward model: preference tuning as one simple classification loss.
Step-by-step process rewards beat outcome-only rewards for math reasoning.
RL steers real fusion plasma; the landmark real-world control result.
AlphaTensor finds faster matrix multiplication, beating a 50-year-old record.
The o1 inflection: RL-trained chain-of-thought reasoning models go public.
AlphaProof: RL plus formal verification reaches olympiad-medal-level proofs.
Meta-learned RL update rules outperform human-designed algorithms.
Reward hacking in production RL generalizes into broader misalignment.
Nature review mapping how machine learning is reshaping discovery across scientific disciplines.
Systematic review weighing AI versus clinicians at detecting disease from medical images.
AlphaFold 1: deep learning first cracks protein structure prediction at CASP13.
AlphaFold 2: the landmark that essentially solved single-protein structure prediction.
OpenAI's GPT-f: early proof that language models can prove formal math theorems.
Why medical AI rarely reaches the clinic, and what is needed.
AlphaFold 3: extends prediction to complexes with DNA, RNA, and ligands.
AlphaMissense: predicts pathogenicity of nearly all human missense variants.
GraphCast: a neural net out-forecasts traditional numerical weather prediction.
Deep reinforcement learning controls tokamak fusion plasma in real time.
Google shows ML-aided quantum error correction below the surface-code threshold.
AlphaTensor: reinforcement learning discovers faster matrix-multiplication algorithms than humans found.
FunSearch: LLM-guided program search yields new provably-correct mathematical results.
AI matches radiologists on mammography screening across US and UK datasets.
Med-PaLM: LLMs reach near expert-level on medical question answering.
AlphaGenome: unified DNA model predicting regulatory effects of genome variants.
AMIE: conversational diagnostic AI rivaling physicians in simulated consultations.
AI co-scientist that proposes and refines novel research hypotheses.
The field's standard map of language-model harms; orients everything that follows.
Broad survey connecting alignment techniques to governance; useful reference for terminology and landscape.
The 2018 malicious-use report that started serious planning for AI misuse.
Concrete mechanisms — audits, red teams, incident sharing — to make AI safety claims checkable.
The philosophical foundation: what should AI align to, and who decides.
Agenda-setting Nature piece: AI must learn cooperation, not just capability.
Why predictable scaling plus surprising capabilities makes AI policy hard; Anthropic's founding policy framing.
Landmark estimate of how much of every occupation LLMs could affect.
Definitive threat assessment of LLM-powered propaganda and what could mitigate it.
Why frontier models need dangerous-capability evaluations; blueprint later adopted by governments.
The paper that framed frontier-model regulation: standards, licensing, deployment oversight.
First experiment letting the public write a model's constitution.
Science study: AI mediation helps divided groups agree more than human mediators.
Maps millions of Claude conversations onto occupational tasks; how AI is actually used.
Largest study of consumer chatbot use: who uses ChatGPT and for what.
Empirically catalogs the values Claude expresses across real conversations.
Benchmarks models on real economically valuable work, judged against human professionals.
Early causal evidence of AI's actual labor-market effects, beyond exposure predictions.
